description: Immerse yourself in the vibrant culture and rich history of London, a city that seamlessly blends the old with the new. Over the course of seven days, you and your family will explore iconic landmarks, indulge in delightful culinary experiences, and dive deep into the world of Sherlock Holmes, all while discovering hidden gems that make this city truly special. From quaint cafes to bustling markets, every moment will be filled with the charm and excitement that London has to offer.

activities.description: Start your day with a delicious breakfast at Dishoom, known for its Bombay-style dishes. Try the bacon naan roll and a cup of chai for a delightful morning treat.
Dive into the world of Sherlock Holmes at the famous museum located at 221B Baker Street. Explore the exhibits dedicated to Arthur Conan Doyle's legendary detective and his adventures.

activities.activity: Lunch at The Sherlock Holmes Pub
activities.description: Enjoy a themed lunch at The Sherlock Holmes Pub, where you can savor traditional British fare in a cozy atmosphere filled with memorabilia from the stories.
Stroll through Covent Garden, a vibrant area filled with shops, street performers, and cafes. Don't forget to check out the market for unique Sherlock-themed souvenirs.
Discover the vast collections at the British Museum, including the Rosetta Stone and Egyptian mummies. Admission is free, making it a perfect budget-friendly activity.
Indulge in a delicious steak dinner at Flat Iron, known for its affordable yet high-quality cuts of meat. The casual atmosphere makes it a great spot for families.
End your day with a relaxing walk along the Thames River. Enjoy the beautiful views of the city skyline and iconic landmarks like the London Eye and Tower Bridge.
Start your day with a hearty breakfast at The Breakfast Club, known for its delicious pancakes and classic English breakfast options. It's a cozy spot that will fuel you for a day of exploration!

Dive into the world of Sherlock Holmes at the famous museum located at 221B Baker Street. Explore the rooms filled with memorabilia and learn about the legendary detective's adventures. Don't forget to snap a photo with the iconic statue outside!

activities.activity: Lunch at The Sherlock Holmes Pub
activities.description: Enjoy a themed lunch at The Sherlock Holmes Pub, where you can savor traditional British dishes in a setting filled with Holmes memorabilia. It's the perfect place to relax and discuss your morning adventures!

Stroll along Baker Street, soaking in the atmosphere of this iconic street. Visit nearby shops for Sherlock-themed merchandise and collectibles, perfect for fans looking to take home a piece of the detective's world.

Take a closer look at the exterior of 221B Baker Street, the fictional home of Sherlock Holmes. The building is a must-see for fans, and you can enjoy the surrounding area, which is rich in history and charm.
Head to the British Museum to explore its vast collection of art and antiquities. While not directly related to Sherlock, the museum's rich history and stunning exhibits will captivate your interest.
End your day with a delightful dinner at Dishoom, a popular Indian restaurant inspired by the old Irani cafés of Bombay. The atmosphere is vibrant, and the food is a treat for your taste buds!
Start your day with a hearty breakfast at The Breakfast Club, known for its delicious pancakes and full English breakfasts. It's a cozy spot that captures the essence of London mornings.

Explore the vast collections at the British Museum, home to artifacts from around the world, including the Rosetta Stone and Egyptian mummies. Admission is free, but consider joining a guided tour for deeper insights.
Enjoy a flavorful lunch at Dishoom, a Bombay-style café that serves up delicious Indian dishes. The ambiance is vibrant, and the food is a delightful fusion of traditional and modern flavors.

Stroll through Covent Garden, a lively area filled with shops, street performers, and cafes. Don't miss the Apple Market for unique crafts and souvenirs, perfect for picking up some Sherlock-themed goodies.
Head to the iconic 221B Baker Street, the fictional home of Sherlock Holmes. The Sherlock Holmes Museum offers a glimpse into the detective's world, complete with memorabilia and exhibits.

activities.activity: Dinner at The Sherlock Holmes Pub
activities.description: Wrap up your day with dinner at The Sherlock Holmes Pub, where you can enjoy classic British dishes in a themed setting. It's a great place to reflect on your day of Sherlock exploration.

Take a leisurely evening stroll through Soho, known for its vibrant nightlife and eclectic atmosphere. It's a great way to unwind after a day of cultural exploration and perhaps find a cozy bar for a nightcap.
Start your day with a hearty breakfast at The Breakfast Club, known for its delicious pancakes and full English breakfast. It's a cozy spot that will fuel you for a day of exploration!

Explore the historic Tower of London, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Discover the Crown Jewels and learn about the fascinating history of this iconic fortress. Don't forget to take a guided tour for the best experience!

Head to Borough Market for lunch, where you can sample a variety of street food from local vendors. Try the famous cheese toasties or fresh seafood, and enjoy the vibrant atmosphere of this bustling market.
Take a leisurely stroll to Tower Bridge, one of London's most famous landmarks. You can walk across the bridge and enjoy stunning views of the Thames River and the city skyline.

Visit the Sherlock Holmes Museum located at 221B Baker Street. Dive into the world of Arthur Conan Doyle's famous detective and explore the exhibits that bring his stories to life. A must-visit for any Sherlock fan!
After the museum, take some time to shop for Sherlock-themed merchandise. There are several shops nearby where you can find unique souvenirs, from books to collectibles.

activities.activity: Dinner at The Sherlock Holmes Pub
activities.description: End your day with dinner at The Sherlock Holmes Pub, a charming establishment filled with memorabilia from the detective's adventures. Enjoy traditional British fare in a cozy atmosphere.
Start your day with a hearty breakfast at The Breakfast Club, known for its delicious pancakes and classic English breakfast options. It's a cozy spot that will fuel you for a day of exploration!
Stroll through Trafalgar Square, a bustling hub of culture and history. Don't forget to take a photo with the iconic Nelson's Column and enjoy the vibrant atmosphere of street performers and artists.
Dive into the world of art at the National Gallery, home to masterpieces by Van Gogh, Da Vinci, and Monet. Admission is free, making it a perfect cultural stop for art lovers.
Enjoy a delightful lunch at Dishoom, a Bombay-style café that serves up delicious Indian dishes. The atmosphere is vibrant, and the food is a must-try, especially the house chai!
Take a leisurely stroll through Hyde Park, one of London's largest and most famous parks. Enjoy the serene landscapes, and if you're lucky, you might catch a glimpse of the local wildlife.
Head to the Sherlock Holmes Museum at 221B Baker Street. Immerse yourself in the world of the famous detective with exhibits and memorabilia that will delight any fan.

activities.activity: Dinner at The Sherlock Holmes Pub
activities.description: Wrap up your day with dinner at The Sherlock Holmes Pub, where you can enjoy traditional British fare in a themed setting. It's a great place to reflect on your day of Sherlock-themed adventures!
After dinner, take some time to shop for Sherlock Holmes merchandise at nearby stores. You'll find everything from books to collectibles that will make perfect souvenirs.
Start your day with a delicious breakfast at Dishoom, known for its Bombay-style dishes. Try the bacon naan roll and a cup of their signature chai for a delightful morning treat.

Dive into the world of Sherlock Holmes at the famous museum located at 221B Baker Street. Explore the rooms and exhibits dedicated to Arthur Conan Doyle's legendary detective, and don't forget to snap a photo with the iconic statue outside.

activities.activity: Lunch at The Sherlock Holmes Pub
activities.description: Enjoy a themed lunch at The Sherlock Holmes Pub, where you can savor traditional British fare in a cozy atmosphere. The pub is adorned with memorabilia from the stories, making it a perfect spot for fans.

Head to Oxford Street, one of the busiest shopping streets in Europe. Browse through a mix of high street brands and unique shops, perfect for picking up some Sherlock-themed merchandise and other souvenirs.

Stroll through Camden Market, known for its eclectic mix of shops and food stalls. Here, you can find unique gifts, vintage clothing, and delicious street food. It's a great place to soak in the vibrant atmosphere of London.

Wrap up your day with dinner in Chinatown, where you can indulge in a variety of Asian cuisines. Try some dim sum or Peking duck at one of the many restaurants, and enjoy the lively ambiance of the area.
Take a leisurely evening stroll along the River Thames. Enjoy the beautiful views of the city skyline and iconic landmarks like the London Eye and Big Ben, a perfect way to end your trip.

transit.memo: Direct flight from Heathrow to D.C.

activities.description: Start your day with a hearty breakfast at The Breakfast Club, known for its delicious pancakes and full English breakfast. It's a cozy spot that will fuel you up for the day ahead!

Explore the iconic home of Sherlock Holmes at 221B Baker Street. The museum is filled with memorabilia and exhibits that bring the famous detective's world to life. Don't forget to take a photo with the famous statue of Sherlock outside!

Join a guided walking tour that takes you through the streets of London, visiting key locations from the Sherlock Holmes stories. It's a fantastic way to immerse yourself in the literary history while enjoying the sights of the city.

activities.activity: Lunch at The Sherlock Holmes Pub
activities.description: Enjoy a themed lunch at The Sherlock Holmes Pub, where you can savor traditional British fare in a setting filled with Sherlock memorabilia. It's a perfect spot to relax and discuss your favorite Holmes stories!
Spend some time shopping for Sherlock-themed merchandise at nearby shops. From books to collectibles, you'll find plenty of unique items to take home as souvenirs of your adventure in London.
